'''Iella Wessiri Antilles''' was a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], who served as a [[Corellian Security Force|CorSec]] [[Police officer/Legends|agent]] and, during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. She went on to become a member of [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]], and later married [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]].

==Biography==
===Corellian Security===
[[File:Corsecuniform.jpg|thumb|left|Wessiri with her CorSec partner, Corran Horn.]]
Iella joined the [[Corellian Security Force]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. Later, she partnered with [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] for two years,<ref name="Krytos" /> acting as a mentor to the headstrong young [[Police officer/Legends|officer]], and also worked with veteran [[Detective/Legends|investigator]] [[Gil Bastra]]. Wessiri and Horn foiled several kidnapping attempts, including ones made on [[Siolle Tinta]] and [[Syal Antilles Fel|Wynssa Starflare]] (although it wasn't until years later that they learned Starflare's true identity). After a harrowing raid on a [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] den, she and Corran nearly slept together. However, [[Kirtan Loor]]—the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Liaison officer—took them in for debriefing, and when they saw each other again, the moment had passed.

Corran learned that Kirtan Loor was planning to have them arrested on trumped up charges, so he, Gil Bastra, Iella Wessiri, and Iella's husband [[Diric Wessiri]], made plans to escape. Corran escaped as Loor was planning on having him murdered. Diric and Iella Wessiri escaped to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

===Joining the Rebellion===
On Coruscant, Iella joined the Rebellion as an intelligence operative, sometimes performing deep undercover work for months at a time. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], she assisted [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in its undercover mission to retake Imperial-occupied [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from [[Ysanne Isard]]'s regime.<ref name="Wedge">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>

Iella and Diric were often separated due to work. Diric was in fact a sleeper agent, captured and brainwashed by Isard during his confinement on the hidden Imperial prison facility ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]''. While Iella was escorting former [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] officer [[Kirtan Loor]] into Republic custody, the two were attacked by an Imperial assassin, who killed Loor before Iella shot him. Tragically, she realized it was actually Diric, who thanked her with his dying breath for releasing him from Isard's control.<ref name="Krytos" />

===The Bacta War===
She participated in the [[Bacta War]] by inserting herself to [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]] along with [[Elscol Loro]] and [[Elscol Loro's anti-Imperial organization|her team]] to organize the local [[Vratix/Legends|Vratix]] in toppling the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]]. Under the the codename of Hook she helped perform many covert operations. One of which was joining a [[Ashern strike team|strike team]] that launched a [[raid on the Dlarit estate]] to humiliate [[Aerin Dlarit]], stripping him of his uniform and spreading [[Hologram/Legends|holos]] of their deed. The planet was eventually liberated after the [[Battle of Thyferra]].<ref name="Bacta">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref>

In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Iella was able to exact justice by fatally shooting Isard during the Rogues' struggle with Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]/[[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Delak Krennel]].<ref name="Isard">''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''</ref>

[[File:Wedge_Iella_Suvudu.jpg|thumb|left|Iella and Wedge, shortly before the [[Battle of Adumar]]]]
During the Rogues' mission to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Wessiri and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] developed a deep mutual attraction. Though she admitted her love for Diric was not passionate, her sense of loyalty prevented her from pursuing a relationship with the Alliance hero.<ref name="Wedge" /> Later, during the [[Zsinj]] campaign after Diric's death, the two began dating when duty assignments converged on Coruscant. Their relationship stalled out due to conflicting careers, only to reignite during a mission to bring the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]] into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Iella used the alias '''Fiana Novarr''' during this undercover mission. Her cover story was that of a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] hired by the [[Cartann]] government to do interfaces between their set of computer protocols and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] standards. Wedge proposed before their time on Adumar was over.<ref name="Starfighters">''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]''</ref>

By the time of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] marriage to [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], it was revealed the two had been married for some time and had two daughters, [[Syal Antilles|Syal]] and [[Myri Antilles|Myri]].<ref name="Union" />
Iella's career with [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] following the Adumar operation is so far undocumented. She retired after the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but, along with Wedge, came out of retirement during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. She and Wedge both retired after the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]].

===Second Galactic Civil War===
In early [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Iella, Wedge, and their youngest daughter, Myri, were living in [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. During her retirement, Iella began grooming Myri to follow in her footsteps as an intelligence agent. When tensions escalated between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and Corellia, Iella and her husband were forced to come out of retirement. Wedge openly sided with Corellia after a failed attempt by the Galactic Alliance to keep him out of the war.<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref> Iella, on the other hand, continued to work as an intelligence agent.<ref name="Exile">''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''</ref>

Following Wedge's resignation from the Corellian military, Iella, Myri, Corran, and Mirax rescued him from several CorSec officers attempting to assassinate him. The group then fled to the ''[[Errant Venture/Legends|Errant Venture]]'', where they reunited with old friends. While on the ship, Iella and Myri worked as dealers in the gambling area in an effort to gather intelligence.<ref name="Exile" />

Iella began analyzing holonews and distributing her conclusions to [[Booster Terrik]], [[Talon Karrde]], her husband, and, when Wedge began working as his military advisor, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. During the [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Centerpoint Station]], she accompanied a Jedi strike team on the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Reveille]]'' in infiltrating the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']], and joined [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] in rescuing [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]], while Luke, [[Saba Sebatyne]], and [[Ben Skywalker]] provided a diversion for [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] by attacking him on the bridge. She escaped with the rest of the strike team on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], piloted by [[Jagged Fel]].<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:IellaAndMara-Union.jpg|thumb|right|Iella after her marriage to Wedge Antilles.]]
{{Quote|No, really, please. I have to know. It normally takes a vote of the Senate or a planetary collision to get Iella to change her mind. I need to learn how to do whatever you did.|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] to [[Hallis Saper]]|<ref name="Starfighters" />}}
Iella herself once said she was pretty stubborn, even more so when she was younger.<ref name="Starfighters" />She was also very competent in almost all things she did, even going as far as saying she could beat Corran where all their skills would overlap.<ref name="Bacta" /> 

===Relationships===
====Corran Horn====
Iella had a close friendship with her Corellian Security Force partner Corran Horn. During one particular mission at a [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] dealer's warehouse, she found herself saving his life. Horn's gratefulness made him feel and think that he was in love with Wessiri, to which she responded similarly. After the mission, they were separated and debriefed without them seeing each other for two days. Horn then realized his attraction had been the result of the emotional comedown from combat. Nothing happened between the two and they continued being friends.<ref name="Rogue">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>

====Diric Wessiri====
She married [[Diric Wessiri]] sometime during her tenure in the Corellian Security Force. While their marriage was not particularly passionate, it was a stable and affectionate one. They soon left Corellia and fled to Coruscant, living under fake identities created by Gil Bastra. Diric was captured on a routine sweep and she then joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. They were reunited when Coruscant was [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberated]] from Isard's control. It turned out that Diric was used in [[Evir Derricote]]'s experiments with the [[Krytos virus]]. She tried nursing him back to health as she contributed to the prosecution team of [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu's]] [[Trial of Tycho Celchu|trial]]. After being assigned to protect Kirtan Loor, who was coming out to testify, she successfully shot the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who killed Loor. It turned out to be none other than Diric, who was a victim of Isard's brain washing on [[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]. He died in her arms.<ref name="Wedge" />

==Skills and abilities==
Iella is an incredible a spy for the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Her analytical skills were incredible to use tracking down criminals, identifying moles, locating the weak spots in an organization she's trying to infiltrate, or figuring out how to lure her enemies into a trap. They can also determine with a very small margin of error how long it'll take for Corran's various relationships to go down in flames. Iella is also incredibly skilled with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], managing to hit her targets from close range or long range.
